steps.title: Rearrange the inequality to identify the solution region
steps.content:
Rearrange $3x - 7y > 21$ by dividing by $-7$ (flipping the inequality):
$$y < \frac{3}{7}x - 3$$
The solution set is all points below the boundary line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$.

steps.title: Locate the boundary line
steps.content:
The line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$ has:
  • y-intercept: $(0, -3)$ — on the negative y-axis
  • x-intercept: Set $y = 0$, then $\frac{3}{7}x = 3$, so $x = 7$, giving $(7, 0)$
  • Slope: $\frac{3}{7} > 0$ — tilts upward from left to right

steps.title: Identify the region with no solutions
steps.content: For the region where $x < 0$ and $y > 0$ (second quadrant): When $x < 0$, the line satisfies $$y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3 < -3 < 0$$ so the line stays below the $x$-axis. Every point with $x < 0$ and $y > 0$ lies above the line. Since solutions require points below the line, the second quadrant contains no solutions. This region is choice A — A is correct.
